basic_design.purpose: Normalize user-provided BD into screen, API, and batch design inputs.

01_Screen.description: Screen/page level scope, actors, UI flow, and UI-driven data needs.

02_API.description: API level scope, external interfaces, and business capabilities.

03_Batch.description: Batch or background processing scope and operational constraints.

detail_design.purpose: Generate implementation-ready DD artifacts for screen, API, and batch modules.

sections.title: UI Design
sections.description: Screen layout, user actions, navigation, and UI assumptions.

sections.title: Components
sections.description: Reusable UI and domain components used by the screen.

sections.title: Data Models
sections.description: Entities, fields, and client-side view models required by the screen.

sections.title: API Integration
sections.description: Backend APIs, request data, response data, and error handling.

sections.title: Business Logic
sections.description: User-facing rules, branching, and workflow logic.

sections.title: State Management
sections.description: UI state, loading/error state, and data refresh behavior.

sections.title: Contract
sections.description: Method, path, headers, query/body schema, responses, and errors.

sections.title: Business Logic
sections.description: Service-level rules, branch conditions, idempotency, and state changes.

sections.title: Data Access
sections.description: Tables, query patterns, indexes, and transaction boundaries.

sections.title: Job Definition
sections.description: Job trigger, schedule, owner, timeout, and retry policy.

sections.title: Processing Logic
sections.description: Step-by-step processing, branching, failure behavior, and observability.

sections.title: Data Access
sections.description: Read/write datasets, checkpoints, and idempotency keys.
